skill.name: gws-sheets-append
skill.description: Append a row to a Google Sheets spreadsheet using the Google Workspace CLI.
skill.summary: Automate adding rows to Google Sheets spreadsheets via the official Google Workspace CLI tool.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged patterns in markdown documentation that are false positives. The backtick characters are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. Path references like ../gws-shared are documentation links, not filesystem traversal attacks. The skill uses the official Google Workspace CLI (gws) for authenticated Sheets operations. External command usage is legitimate and expected for a CLI wrapper skill.

low_findings.title: External Command Usage
low_findings.description: Skill invokes the gws CLI tool for Google Sheets operations. This is expected behavior for a CLI wrapper but requires user authentication setup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The gws CLI is the official Google Workspace command-line tool - legitimate external dependency for this skill's purpose.

content.user_title: Append rows to Google Sheets spreadsheets
content.value_statement: Manually adding rows to spreadsheets consumes valuable time. This skill automates row appending to Google Sheets using the official Google Workspace CLI.
actual_capabilities: Append single rows to Google Sheets with comma-separated values
Append multiple rows using JSON array format
Target specific sheet tabs using range notation
Support both simple and structured data formats
limitations: Requires Google Workspace CLI (gws) to be installed and authenticated
Cannot read or modify existing cells - only append new rows
Limited to spreadsheet operations within user's Google Workspace permissions
Requires spreadsheet ID to be known beforehand

use_cases.title: Automated Data Logging
use_cases.description: Log timestamped entries to a tracking spreadsheet for audit trails or monitoring.

use_cases.title: Survey Response Collection
use_cases.description: Automatically append survey responses to a master spreadsheet for analysis.

use_cases.title: Inventory Updates
use_cases.description: Add new inventory items to a shared tracking spreadsheet.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single Row Append
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new row to my spreadsheet with values: John, 500, Active
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick data entry for a single record

prompt_templates.title: Append to Specific Sheet Tab
prompt_templates.prompt: Append the values Sales,Q1,25000 to the Summary tab of my quarterly report spreadsheet
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeting a specific tab within a multi-tab spreadsheet

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Multi-Row Insert
prompt_templates.prompt: Add these three rows to my inventory sheet: Widget,100,USD and Gadget,50,EUR and Tool,75,GBP
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding multiple records in a single operation

prompt_templates.title: Structured JSON Data Import
prompt_templates.prompt: Import this data to my leads spreadsheet: [[Name,Company,Value],[Acme Corp,Tech,5000],[Globex,Finance,8000]]
prompt_templates.scenario: Importing structured tabular data with headers

output_examples.input: Append row with values: Task Complete,2024-01-15,John
output_examples.output: Successfully appended 1 row to spreadsheet. Values added to the next available row in the active sheet.

output_examples.input: Append JSON values [[Product,Qty,Price]] to range Inventory!A1
output_examples.output: Successfully appended 1 row to Inventory tab at range A1. Spreadsheet updated with new product entry.

best_practices: Always confirm the spreadsheet ID before executing append operations to avoid writing to the wrong document
Use JSON format for multi-row inserts to ensure data consistency and reduce API calls
Verify user authentication status before attempting write operations to prevent permission errors
anti_patterns: Appending sensitive data like passwords or API keys to spreadsheets without encryption
Using append operations for data that requires validation against existing rows
Automating frequent appends without rate limiting, which may trigger API quotas

faq.question: What authentication is required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need the Google Workspace CLI (gws) installed and authenticated with your Google account. Run gws auth to set up credentials.

faq.question: How do I find my spreadsheet ID?
faq.answer: The spreadsheet ID is the long string in the URL between /d/ and /edit. For example, in https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/ABC123xyz/edit, the ID is ABC123xyz.

faq.question: Can this skill modify existing cells?
faq.answer: No, this skill only appends new rows. To modify existing cells, use the gws-sheets-update skill instead.

faq.question: What is the difference between --values and --json-values?
faq.answer: Use --values for a single row with comma-separated values. Use --json-values for multiple rows formatted as a JSON array of arrays.

faq.question: How do I append to a specific sheet tab?
faq.answer: Use the --range flag with the tab name followed by an exclamation mark and cell reference, such as Sheet2!A1 for the second tab.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many rows I can append at once?
faq.answer: Google Sheets has a 5 million cell limit per spreadsheet. For bulk operations, consider batching large inserts to avoid timeout issues.
